root_text.sn45.159:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, āgantukāgāraṁ. Tattha puratthimāyapi disāya āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, pacchimāyapi disāya āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, uttarāyapi disāya āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, dakkhiṇāyapi disāya āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, khattiyāpi āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, brāhmaṇāpi āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, vessāpi āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti, suddāpi āgantvā vāsaṁ kappenti;
root_text.sn45.159:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvento ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaronto ye dhammā abhiññā pariññeyyā, te dhamme abhiññā parijānāti …pe…
root_text.sn45.159:1.3: ye dhammā abhiññā pahātabbā, te dhamme abhiññā pajahati, ye dhammā abhiññā sacchikātabbā, te dhamme abhiññā sacchikaroti, ye dhammā abhiññā bhāvetabbā, te dhamme abhiññā bhāveti.
root_text.sn45.159:2.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā abhiññā pariññeyyā?
root_text.sn45.159:2.6: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā abhiññā pahātabbā?
root_text.sn45.159:2.9: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā abhiññā sacchikātabbā?
root_text.sn45.159:2.12: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā abhiññā bhāvetabbā?
root_text.sn45.159:2.15: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvento ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaronto, ye dhammā abhiññā pariññeyyā te dhamme abhiññā parijānāti …pe… ye dhammā abhiññā bhāvetabbā, te dhamme abhiññā bhāveti?
root_text.sn45.159:2.16: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …
root_text.sn45.159:2.17: evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvento ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaronto ye dhammā abhiññā pariññeyyā, te dhamme abhiññā parijānāti, ye dhammā abhiññā pahātabbā, te dhamme abhiññā pajahati, ye dhammā abhiññā sacchikātabbā, te dhamme abhiññā sacchikaroti, ye dhammā abhiññā bhāvetabbā, te dhamme abhiññā bhāvetī”ti.

translation_text.sn45.159:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose there was a guest house. Lodgers come from the east, west, north, and south. Aristocrats, brahmins, peasants, and menials all stay there.
translation_text.sn45.159:1.2: In the same way, a mendicant who develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path completely understands by direct knowledge the things that should be completely understood by direct knowledge.
translation_text.sn45.159:1.3: They give up by direct knowledge the things that should be given up by direct knowledge. They realize by direct knowledge the things that should be realized by direct knowledge. They develop by direct knowledge the things that should be developed by direct knowledge.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.1: And what are the things that should be completely understood by direct knowledge?
translation_text.sn45.159:2.2: It should be said: the five grasping aggregates.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.4: That is: form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.5: These are the things that should be completely understood by direct knowledge.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.6: And what are the things that should be given up by direct knowledge?
translation_text.sn45.159:2.7: Ignorance and craving for continued existence.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.8: These are the things that should be given up by direct knowledge.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.9: And what are the things that should be realized by direct knowledge?
translation_text.sn45.159:2.11: These are the things that should be realized by direct knowledge.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.12: And what are the things that should be developed by direct knowledge?
translation_text.sn45.159:2.14: These are the things that should be developed by direct knowledge.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.15: And how does a mendicant develop the noble eightfold path in this way?
translation_text.sn45.159:2.16: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn45.159:2.17: That’s how a mendicant develops and cultivates the eightfold path in this way.”
